config.content:
## Agent Memory — Overview
**What this is**: Real-time view of what your Claude Code agents are storing, doing, and remembering across sessions and machines.
**Look for**: Memory growth rate · Which agents are most active · What action types dominate sessions · Unread messages between agents · Entity index health
**Tip**: Narrow time range to Last 24h for recent activity. A spike in sessions often means a long agentic run. Rising memory count means agents are retaining context successfully.

config.title: Memories
config.description: Total memories stored across all agents. Includes decisions, handoffs, references, and notes.

config.title: Sessions
config.description: Total session events logged. Each agent action (task start, file write, recall, etc.) creates a session record.

config.title: Active Tasks
config.description: Tasks currently in progress. Should be 0 or 1 per active agent — a high number indicates stale task state.

data_source.query: FROM agent-tasks | WHERE status == "in_progress" | STATS `Active Tasks` = COUNT(*)

config.title: Completed Tasks
config.description: Tasks finished successfully with an outcome recorded. Tracks productive agent work over the selected time range.

data_source.query: FROM agent-tasks | WHERE status == "completed" | STATS `Completed Tasks` = COUNT(*)

config.title: Entities
config.description: Markdown files indexed for semantic search. Grows as agents write or edit .md files in BRIDGE_WATCH_DIRS.

config.title: Unread Messages
config.description: Messages in the inter-agent queue not yet acknowledged. Non-zero means an agent has pending communication to act on.

data_source.query: FROM agent-messages | WHERE status == "unread" | STATS `Unread Messages` = COUNT(*)

config.title: Sessions Over Time
config.description: Session events per time bucket. Spikes = high-activity or long agentic runs. Flat periods = no agent running.

data_source.query: FROM agent-sessions | WHERE timestamp <= ?_tend AND timestamp > ?_tstart | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY BUCKET(timestamp, 75, ?_tstart, ?_tend)

config.title: Memories Created
config.description: New memories stored per time bucket. Rising trend = agents persisting more context across sessions.

data_source.query: FROM agent-memory | WHERE created_at <= ?_tend AND created_at > ?_tstart | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY BUCKET(created_at, 75, ?_tstart, ?_tend)

config.title: Memory by Type
config.description: Distribution of stored memory types. Common types: handoff, project, feedback, reference, observation.

data_source.query: FROM agent-memory | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY memory_type = type | SORT count DESC

config.title: Entities by Type
config.description: Entity index breakdown by type, inferred from directory structure in BRIDGE_WATCH_DIRS (e.g. docs/, notes/, projects/).

data_source.query: FROM *-entities | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY entity_type | SORT count DESC

config.title: Tasks by Status
config.description: Task lifecycle distribution. Suspended tasks may need attention. Failed tasks indicate errors worth investigating.

data_source.query: FROM agent-tasks | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY status | SORT count DESC

config.title: Memories by Agent
config.description: Memory volume per agent ID. Multi-agent setups show multiple bars. Imbalance may indicate one agent doing more persistent work.

data_source.query: FROM agent-memory | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY agent | SORT count DESC

config.title: Sessions by Action
config.description: Most frequent session action types logged. token-turn dominates active AI assistant usage. task-status-change tracks task lifecycle events.

data_source.query: FROM agent-sessions | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY action | SORT count DESC | LIMIT 10
